title: North America drives growth at Computacenter
author: Simon Quicke
full_text: North America drives growth at Computacenter Channel player shares numbers for the 2025 financial year, with the UK and Germany solid – but there is higher growth coming from other regions Computacenters strategy to expand the business on the other side of the Atlantic over the past few years has been highlighted as a wise one, with the region delivering an outstanding year for the cha…
summary: Computacenter's North American business drove growth for the company, with higher expansion coming from the region. The UK and Germany had solid years, but North America led the way. The company's strategy to expand in North America has been deemed successful. The region's performance was highlighted as outstanding for the company. The financial year in question is 2025.

summary: Asustek Computer reported a record full-year net profit of NT$44.56 billion (approximately US$1.40 billion) for 2025, a 42% year-over-year increase. The company's profit was driven by its artificial intelligence (AI) server business. Asustek's server revenue is expected to double in the first quarter of 2026. The company's financial results benefit from the growing demand for AI servers. Asustek's profit is a new record for the company.

title: IDF Spokesman: 'Your AI is as stupid as you are'
author: Israel National News
full_text: IDF Spokesman: 'Your AI is as stupid as you are' IDF Arabic spokesman Avichay Adraee denounced a Hezbollah video of a missile strike in Israel as a digital forgery. The IDFs Arabic-language spokesperson, Avichay Adraee, said that a viral video shared online claiming to show an Iranian missile striking central Tel Aviv is actually a digitally manipulated clip. T…
summary: IDF Arabic spokesperson Avichay Adraee has denounced a Hezbollah video of a missile strike in Israel as a digital forgery. The video claimed to show an Iranian missile striking central Tel Aviv, but Adraee says it is a manipulated clip. Adraee also made a personal attack on Hezbollah, calling their AI "as stupid as you are". The Israeli military is accusing Hezbollah of spreading false information. The authenticity of the video is being disputed.

title: From digital access to learning outcomes: India’s edtech emphasis needs to evolve
author: Amitabh Kant, Gouri Gupta
full_text: From digital access to learning outcomes: India’s edtech emphasis needs to evolve The Bharat Survey for EdTech shows that digital access is now widespread in India, but technologies aren’t fully understood. The focus of edtech must shift from access to structured learning and improving outcomes to deliver the human capital gains we need fo… This ambition rests on human capital. Sustained economic growth will depend not only on infrastructure and innovation, but on workforce productivity. At its foundation, this means ensuring that ever…
summary: The Bharat Survey for EdTech has found that digital access is widespread in India, but technology understanding is lacking. The focus of edtech needs to shift from access to improving learning outcomes. Economic growth in India depends on workforce productivity, which relies on human capital. To achieve this, India must ensure that education is structured to deliver better outcomes. The current emphasis on access must evolve to prioritize learning outcomes.

summary: A documentary examines how Tesla drivers are essentially unpaid beta testers for the company's self-driving cars. Elon Musk's focus on long-term goals, including colonizing Mars, has raised concerns about the safety of the cars. Musk has topped Forbes' Billionaires list for the second year in a row. He is on track to become the world's first trillionaire. His wealth and ambition have led to criticism about the risks of his business practices.

title: Elon Musk's Tesla experiment: Unchecked ambition costs lives
author: Elizabeth Grenier
full_text: Elon Musk's Tesla experiment: Unchecked ambition costs lives A documentary explores how Tesla drivers serve as unpaid beta testers of the company's self-driving cars while Elon Musk focuses on his long-term goal: colonizing Mars. Elon Musk, who this week topped Forbes' Billionaires list for the second year in a row, is on course to become the world's first trillionaire. On top of his extreme wealth, Musk is bound to remain i…
summary: A documentary criticizes Tesla's approach to self-driving cars, suggesting that drivers are acting as unpaid beta testers while the company focuses on its long-term goals. The film argues that this unchecked ambition has led to accidents and lost lives. Tesla drivers are essentially testing the company's technology, putting themselves and others at risk. Elon Musk's focus on colonizing Mars has led to concerns about the company's priorities. Musk's wealth and ambitions are said to be unmatched, with him set to become the world's first trillionaire.

title: Social media giants have grand plans for AI. Creators fear they'll be left out.
author: Sydney Bradley,Dan Whateley
full_text:
Social media giants have grand plans for AI. Creators fear they'll be left out. Social apps like Instagram and TikTok have their attention set on AI, and it's making some creators feel like an afterthought. Getty Images; Tyler Le/BI
  • Influencers are reckoning with a new boogeyman: artificial intelligence.
  • Social apps seem infatuated with AI, and it's making some creators feel like an aft…
summary: Social media platforms like Instagram and TikTok are prioritizing AI development, leaving some creators feeling overlooked. Influencers are concerned about being replaced by AI-generated content. The shift towards AI has sparked worries among creators that they will be left behind. Social media platforms are investing heavily in AI, which is set to change the way content is created and shared. Creators are unsure what the future holds for them. This new development has created uncertainty among influencers and social media creators.

full_text: Do ferrets wash themselves? Do Ferrets Wash Themselves? Unveiling the Grooming Habits of Fuzzy Noodles While ferrets engage in some self-grooming, they do not completely wash themselves like cats. Therefore, owners must assist with bathing and other hygiene practices. Understanding Ferr… While ferrets engage in some self-grooming, they do not completely wash themselves like cats. Therefore, owners must assist with bathing and other hygiene practices. Understanding Ferret Grooming Ha…
summary: Ferrets engage in some self-grooming, but they do not completely wash themselves like cats. They require assistance from their owners for bathing and other hygiene practices. Ferrets do not have the ability to fully clean themselves like some other animals. Their owners must intervene to keep them clean. Self-grooming is a part of a ferret's routine, but it's not enough on its own.
